message.content:
Try HARD to REFUTE this lever. Default refuted=true if uncertain. Check the artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board and the code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

LEVER: Grammatical subject-position in-prose attribution (the ONLY cleaner-surviving form) (L2)
RUBRIC CRITERION: "Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized" (w=0.15 of comprehensiveness) + "Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles'" (w=0.15 of instruction)
CHANGE: The brief's deterministic marker->parenthetical transform is DEAD (probe variant B: cleaner retained zero authors/venues/years, byte-identical to the [n] baseline; every '(YYYY)' is deleted 281->0). Only probe variant C survives: author as grammatical subject — 'Acemoglu and Restrepo, writing in the *Journal of Economic Perspectives*, show that...'. So this is a WRITER-PROMPT change, not a post-pass: add a rule to SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_FIELD_AGNOSTIC that when an evidence row carries verified authors+venue, the finding is written with the author as subject/possessive and the venue as an in-clause appositive; do NOT spend tokens on years (100% deletion rate). Keep [ev_XXX] markers (stripped harmlessly). Post-verify: every injected author/venue token must be a byte-substring of the enriched sidecar record for that evidence_id, else strip the clause or drop the sentence.
WHERE: composer — /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:3764 (SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE_FIELD_AGNOSTIC, new rule after rule 2 at ~:3770); verifier extension beside provenance_generator.py:2060 (author-bind helper) and compose script :104
CLAIMED: +0.008 to +0.012 overall (capped: the reference article itself has near-zero in-prose attribution and dalpha scores 0.5252 with bare surnames — this pays into ~4.4% of overall, not the brief's imagined 6+%)
ADMITTED FAILURE: The LLM writes attributions for rows lacking metadata (fabrication — must be caught and stripped by the byte-substring verifier); or it writes them in appositive/parenthetical positions the cleaner still deletes (cellcog's own Table 1 citation column was emptied); or attribution density reads as name-dropping and taxes readability L1.
CONTRACT: Two-lane contract, zero-fabrication preserved by construction. LANE A (world-facts): unchanged — every sentence asserting a fact about the world carries [ev_XXX] tokens and passes verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098) with the NEUTRAL clause (entailment_judge.py:588) intact. LANE B (adjudication): a sentence tagged [syn] by the writer is exempt from span entailment and admitted iff it introduces NO new fact — enforced by a new verify_synthesis_sentence beside the existing gate: (i) DETERMINISTIC closed-world check: every digit-bearing token, proper noun, and named mechanism in the [syn] sentence must already appear verbatim in this section's ADMITTED lane-A prose or the bibliography (cellcog's winning synthesis — 'The three frameworks are complementary rather than competitive... layered lenses to be deployed according to the question at hand' — passes this check by inspection: no new number, entity, or fact; only ranking/relating ideas already on the page); (ii) FAIL-CLOSED LLM check: 'does this sentence assert any empirical claim about the world not already asserted in the FACTS block above, or does it only rank, relate, reconcile, or evaluate them?' — any parse error, timeout, or uncertain verdict = drop the sentence. LANE B-NEG (negative-space claims): sentences of the form 'no peer-reviewed study in this corpus has yet X' are claims about the CORPUS, not the world, and are verified mechanically against the evidence pool + bibliography (they are also immune to the NEUTRAL clause by construction — they introduce no mechanism into any span). ATTRIBUTION: author/venue strings injected into prose must be byte-substrings of the ENRICHED sidecar record for the cited evidence_id (extending _audit_citations at compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:104), and the sidecar itself is spot-verified against source pages before any compose — the current sidecar fabricates venues ('Review of AI', wrong years) and MUST NOT be injected until repaired. Red-team acceptance: a 30-sentence set (15 genuine adjudications, 15 with one smuggled fact/number/entity each) must show >=12/15 genuine admitted and 0/15 smuggled admitted, else Lane B ships disabled and we fall back to lane-A-only compose.

ATTACK THROUGH THIS LENS:
TUNNEL VISION (the lens that caught you last time): is this plan silently CONSTRAINED by an assumption nobody tested? Specifically: does it assume the banked artifact is the substrate? does it assume a length cap? does it assume a breadth-vs-depth trade-off? does it assume our current retrieval is what we must write from? **Name the assumption the plan does not know it is making.**

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

---
[
 {
  "sev": "CRITICAL_serializes",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:5468 (and :5514)",
  "title": "The 0615bc5 missing-await bug EXISTS AGAIN at a hotter call site: _compose_section_per_basket is a sync def called with no await/to_thread from the async _run_section \u2014 it blocks the event loop for the ENTIRE per-basket compose of a section",
  "mechanism": "`_compose_section_per_basket` is defined as a plain `def` (verified_compose.py:3262), not `async def`. `_run_section` (multi_section_generator.py:5182) is `async def`. At :5468 it is called as `_vc_composed = _compose_section_per_basket(...)` \u2014 no `await`, no `asyncio.to_thread`. This function is the MAIN per-basket compose loop: it walks every basket in the section, runs writer_fn + verify_fn + the bounded repair loop (redraft_fn) for each. All of it executes synchronously ON the asyncio event loop. Consequence: while section A is inside it, the single event loop is pinned \u2014 section B's coroutine cannot resume, and no in-flight writer/judge HTTP response can be processed. PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 is therefore fiction: the two 'parallel' sections are serialized. The same file already uses `await asyncio.to_thread(strict_verify, ...)` at :5667 and :10288, so the authors knew the pattern \u2014 :5468 and :5514 were simply missed, exactly as _draft_passes_wrapper was.",
  "fix": "Wrap both call sites: `_vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(functools.partial(_compose_section_per_basket, _vc_baskets, evidence_pool, writer_fn=..., verify_fn=..., redraft_fn=..., ...))`. This is the identical remedy applied in 0615bc5. Note it must be done TOGETHER with the redraft fix below, otherwise the offloaded thread still spins a fresh loop per redraft."
 },
 {
  "sev": "CRITICAL_serializes",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:4994",
  "title": "_redraft spins a FRESH asyncio event loop per call, which makes get_semaphore() destroy and recreate the module-global LLM concurrency semaphore \u2014 303 times in one run. PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM is not enforced at all.",
  "mechanism": "`_redraft` (the group re-draft closure fed into _compose_one_basket's bounded repair loop) is a sync function. At :4993-4994 it creates a new `ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1)` and does `fut = _ex.submit(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))` \u2014 a BRAND NEW event loop for every single re-draft. Inside `_run()`, `_call_writer` -> `OpenRouterClient._call_api` (openrouter_client.py:1890) calls `get_semaphore()`. get_semaphore (llm_provider.py:149) guards on `_LLM_SEMAPHORE_LOOP is not running_loop` and, on mismatch, REBINDS the module-global `_LLM_SEMAPHORE = asyncio.Semaphore(cap)`. Because the redraft loop is never the main compose loop, every alternation main-loop -> redraft-loop -> main-loop destroys the semaphore TWICE. Permits held by calls already in flight on the old object simply vanish; the fresh semaphore starts at full cap. So the global cap of 30 bounds nothing, and burst concurrency is unbounded \u2014 which is what produces the 429/connection-error storm. Separately, `fut.result(timeout=call_deadline_s + 30.0)` at :4996 is a BLOCKING wait of up to 330s, and because of finding 1 it executes on the event loop.",
  "fix": "Make _redraft async (`async def _redraft`) and await `_call_writer` directly on the caller's loop \u2014 the ThreadPoolExecutor+asyncio.run sandwich exists only because the caller chain is sync. If it must stay sync, pass the running loop in and use `asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(_run(), main_loop)` instead of `asyncio.run(_run())`. Independently, harden llm_provider.get_semaphore: key the semaphore in a `WeakKeyDictionary[loop -> Semaphore]` instead of clobbering one module global, so a second loop cannot destroy the first loop's cap."
 },
 {
  "sev": "CRITICAL_serializes",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/llm/judge_concurrency.py:178 (acquired at src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:150)",
  "title": "Every NLI judge POST is gated by a process-global BoundedSemaphore(4) \u2014 acquired ON the event loop thread",
  "mechanism": "`_post_with_total_deadline` opens with `with _acquire_judge_slot():` BEFORE submitting the POST. `acquire_judge_slot` resolves PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY, which is set in NO launch script, so it falls to DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4, and does a blocking `sem.acquire()` on a process-global threading.BoundedSemaphore. Two consequences: (a) at most 4 entailment POSTs are in flight process-wide no matter that PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY=12 and PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16 \u2014 the fixed pre-pass's 12 verify threads immediately queue 8-deep behind a cap of 4, so the 0615bc5 speedup is itself capped; (b) on the compose leg (finding 1) the thread doing sem.acquire() IS the event loop, so slot-queueing time is loop-freeze time on top of POST time.",
  "fix": "Set PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY explicitly in the launch script (it was sized at 4 for a 4-host mirror chain during a 429 storm; with the compose leg off-loop, raise it to match PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM and watch 429 rate). Independently: never call acquire_judge_slot from the loop thread."
 },
 {
  "sev": "HIGH",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py:1166 (and :1209)",
  "title": "time.sleep() rate-limit backoff (15-60s) inside the judge retry runs on the event loop thread",
  "mechanism": "`_EntailmentJudge.judge()` (:740) is fully synchronous. On a retryable fault it computes `_sleep_s = _rate_limit_backoff_s(...)` and calls `time.sleep(_sleep_s)` \u2014 not asyncio.sleep. For a 429/503/DNS fault the backoff is floored at PG_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_FLOOR_S=15.0 and capped at PG_ENTAILMENT_RATE_LIMIT_CAP_S=60.0, with a LARGER retry budget than the ordinary path. Reached from the compose leg (finding 1), that time.sleep executes on the asyncio event loop: one 429 on one sentence freezes the ENTIRE report generation \u2014 all sections, all pre-pass writer calls \u2014 for 15-60s, repeatedly. The module's own docstring says 429 storms on the mirror-chain lead host are the expected steady state.",
  "fix": "Getting the compose leg off-loop (finding 1) makes these sleeps block a worker thread, which is correct. Do not leave any path where judge() can be entered from the loop thread."
 },
 {
  "sev": "CRITICAL_serializes",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:5468 (2nd call site :5514); loop body verified_compose.py:3325",
  "title": "_compose_section_per_basket is a SYNC blocking basket loop called with no await/to_thread from the async _run_section - it serializes every per-basket NLI judge AND freezes the whole event loop (all other sections)",
  "mechanism": "_run_section is `async def`. At :5468 it calls `_vc_composed = _compose_section_per_basket(...)` as a plain function call. inspect.iscoroutinefunction(_compose_section_per_basket) == False. Its body loops `for basket in (section_baskets or [])` (verified_compose.py:3325) and, per basket, calls _compose_one_basket / compose_basket_multicited_synth_primary / compose_companion_figure_units (nested member x sentence-unit loop at :3414), each of which calls the SYNC verify_fn(sentence, scoped_pool) (verified_compose.py:2222, 2583, 2981, 3071, 3192) -> make_writer_verify_fn -> verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2154, sync) -> entailment_judge.judge() (sync) -> _post_with_total_deadline -> fut.result(timeout) which BLOCKS THE CALLING THREAD. The calling thread is the asyncio event loop. So (a) all per-basket verification in a section runs one judge POST at a time, and (b) for the entire duration the event loop is frozen, so every OTHER section's writer tasks, timeouts and HTTP callbacks make ZERO progress. This is exactly the 0615bc5 bug class, one level up, unfixed.",
  "fix": "Wrap both call sites in `await asyncio.to_thread(_compose_section_per_basket, ...)` (unblocks the loop immediately, same as 0615bc5), then go further: make the per-basket loop fan out - either an async _compose_section_per_basket that gathers per-basket coroutines under a semaphore, or a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor over baskets with a copied contextvars context (the pattern already written for strict_verify at provenance_generator.py:3676-3718). Baskets are independent; verdicts are order-independent and are reassembled by index."
 },
 {
  "sev": "CRITICAL_serializes",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:5448-5804",
  "title": "The post-draft tail is ~85-88% of every section's wall clock and has NO fan-out at all - the parallel writer pre-pass is only ~12-15% of the work",
  "mechanism": "The execution graph per section is: parallel pre-pass (:5448, Semaphore(24)) -> then a strictly serial chain of five blocking/serial stages: _compose_section_per_basket (:5468), _repair_untokened_draft (:5575), _coherence_rewrite (:5591), strict_verify (:5667), sentence repair (:5693), optional regen + a SECOND strict_verify (:5775/:5804). None of these stages pipelines with the next, and none of them fans out over sentences/baskets. So raising PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY / PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM can only ever compress the 12-15% slice; the other 85% is immune to every concurrency knob in the launch scripts.",
  "fix": "Parallelize inside the tail (see the other findings: basket loop, strict_verify workers, coherence re-verify, sentence repair) and/or pipeline it: start the next section's pre-pass while the previous section's verify tail runs - which becomes automatic once the tail stops blocking the event loop."
 },
 {
  "sev": "CRITICAL_serializes",
  "where": "scripts/run_s5_i3.py:491 (sec_sema) and :588-597 (task creation order)",
  "title": "94% of the baskets live in ONE section coroutine ('Additional Corroborated Findings', 304-326 of 346), and it is scheduled LAST behind a Semaphore(2)",
  "mechanism": "plans=4 -> 5 sections. Sections 0-3 carry 15-18 baskets each; section 4 carries 304-326. sec_sema = Semaphore(PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS) = 2 (iter4/iter5 launchers) and tasks are created in plan order, so section 4 only acquires the semaphore after two of the four small sections complete - i.e. it STARTS ~50-90 minutes into the run. Because the section is the unit of parallelism and its whole compose+verify tail is the single-threaded loop of finding #1, 94% of the work has an achieved parallelism of exactly 1. PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS cannot help: there is no second big section to overlap with.",
  "fix": "Two independent fixes: (1) shard the mega-section's baskets into N chunks composed by N concurrent tasks (the basket loop is already order-stable and dedup is done on the concatenated list, so chunking + in-order reassembly is safe); (2) schedule sections longest-first (sort tasks by len(section.ev_ids) descending) so the mega-section starts at t=0 and overlaps with all the small ones instead of being tail-scheduled."
 },
 {
  "sev": "HIGH",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:3664-3676 (gate) / :3383-3392 (default)",
  "title": "strict_verify's per-sentence entailment loop runs SERIAL because PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is never set - the bounded thread pool exists and is dead code in production",
  "mechanism": "_parallel_verify_workers() returns 1 when PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is unset/empty (:3390-3392). strict_verify then takes the serial branch at :3666-3676 ('if _verify_workers < 2 ... byte-identical to the legacy loop'), running one blocking entailment-judge POST per sentence, sequentially. The ThreadPoolExecutor path at :3678-3718 - which already handles contextvars copying and cost-ledger reconciliation - is never entered. strict_verify is invoked twice per section (:5667 and, on regen, :5804). grep shows PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY appears in NO launch_compose_gear_*.sh and in no os.environ.setdefault in run_s5_i3.py (which does setdefault 5 other flags at :342-359).",
  "fix": "export PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8 (or setdefault it in run_s5_i3.py next to the other Fix-3/Fix-6 setdefaults at :342-359). Zero code change; the parallel path is already written, tested and cost-reconciled."
 },
 {
  "sev": "HIGH",
  "where": "src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py:271-283",

input.command: cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel && ls sota_review workspace 2>/dev/null | head -60; echo ---; cat drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv 2>/dev/null || cat /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""STEP 15: compose the REAL scoreable multi-section report from the LIVE agentic run.

The mission's metric-(a) full-corpus gate (cp4_used=agentic on the 329-basket corpus) already
PASSED (docs/agentic_sweep_live_summary_s3gear329.json). What was missing was a *composed*
report we can score. This driver closes that gap: it runs the FULL generator
(``generate_multi_section_report``) with the agentic outliner ON (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 + the
§9.1.8 model lock) over data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json and writes report.md.

Model it on scripts/run_honest_on_prerebuild_corpus.py (which already produced report.md +
multi_section_outline.json), minus the retrieval/scope machinery (the corpus is pre-built).

Faithfulness gate (HARD): after composition, assert ZERO unverified numbers reach any
[CITE:ev_xxx] token in the composed report. The strict_verify lane already enforces this
per-section; this driver re-audits the final assembled text as an independent tripwire.

Run (key MUST be in env):
    set -a && . ./.env && set +a
    PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1 python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py \
        --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.json
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import asyncio
import hashlib
import json
import logging
import os
import random
import re
import sys
import time
from pathlib import Path
from types import SimpleNamespace

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))

DRB_QUERY = ROOT / "third_party" / "deep_research_bench" / "data" / "prompt_data" / "query.jsonl"

# STEP 2 (wheel: topic-driven structure) — the section headings are now produced TOPIC-DRIVEN by
# the generator itself (facet outline + general research-report skeleton: PG_FACET_OUTLINE=1 +
# PG_FACET_OUTLINE_SKELETON=1). The prior STEP-16 approach hardcoded a clinical-archetype ->
# AI/labor relabel MAP here — an overfit band-aid tuned to one benchmark task. That map is GONE:
# the outliner emits real topical titles (Introduction / thematic bodies / Cross-Study Synthesis /
# Conclusions and Research Gaps) for ANY domain, so assembly renders the section titles verbatim.


def _derive_title(rq: str) -> str:
    """Derive a neutral report title from the research question — GENERAL, not tuned to any task.

    Takes the first sentence/clause of the RQ, strips a leading imperative ("Please write a ...",
    "Research ...", "I am researching ..."), and Title-cases nothing (keeps the RQ's own wording).
    Falls back to a generic label. No topic is hardcoded."""
    import re as _re
    s = (rq or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
    s = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s)
    # First sentence only.
    s = _re.split(r"(?<=[.?!])\s", s, maxsplit=1)[0]
    # Strip common leading imperatives so the title reads as a subject, not a command.
    s = _re.sub(r"^(please\s+)?(help me\s+)?(write|prepare|produce|conduct|research(ing)?|"
                r"provide|create|complete|collect( and)?( organi[sz]e)?|i am researching|"
                r"i would like|i need)\b[:,]?\s*", "", s, flags=_re.IGNORECASE)
    s = s.strip().rstrip(".").strip()
    if not s:
        return "Research Report"
    # Capitalize the first letter only (preserve proper-noun casing in the rest).
    return s[0].upper() + s[1:]


def _load_drb_prompt(task_id: str) -> str:
    """Load a DeepResearch-Bench task's EXACT prompt verbatim (target/ref/criteria all key on it)."""
    for line in DRB_QUERY.read_text().splitlines():
        o = json.loads(line)
        if str(o.get("id")) == str(task_id):
            return o["prompt"]
    raise SystemExit(f"BLOCKED: DRB task id {task_id} not in {DRB_QUERY}")

logging.basicConfig(
    level=os.environ.get("PG_LOG_LEVEL", "INFO"),
    format="%(asctime)s %(name)s %(levelname)s %(message)s",
    datefmt="%H:%M:%S",
    stream=sys.stdout,
)
for noisy in ("httpx", "httpcore"):
    logging.getLogger(noisy).setLevel(logging.WARNING)
log = logging.getLogger("compose")


def _tier_fractions(evidence: list[dict]) -> dict[str, float]:
    from collections import Counter
    c = Counter((e.get("tier") or "T?").upper() for e in evidence)
    n = sum(c.values()) or 1
    return {k: v / n for k, v in sorted(c.items())}


# A numeric token that would be a faithfulness breach if it sat inside a [CITE:] sentence
# without having passed strict_verify. We audit the FINAL assembled report: any [CITE:ev_xxx]
# in the verified text is, by construction, already span-grounded — but we re-scan to prove it.
_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[0-9a-fA-F]+|[a-z0-9_]+)\]")


def _audit_citations(report_text: str, biblio: list[dict]) -> dict:
    """Independent faithfulness tripwire on the FINAL assembled report.

    strict_verify resolves every kept sentence's provenance token into a global [N] bibliography
    marker and DROPS any sentence whose number failed the span match. So in a faithful final
    report: (1) ZERO raw [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens survive (any survivor is an unverified-number leak
    — the exact breach the mission forbids), and (2) every [N] marker in the prose resolves to a
    real bibliography entry. We assert both."""
    leaked_cites = _CITE_RE.findall(report_text)
    body = report_text.split("\n\n## References\n", 1)[0]  # markers in prose only
    n_markers = set(int(m) for m in re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", body))
    biblio_nums = {int(b.get("num")) for b in biblio if str(b.get("num", "")).isdigit()}
    unresolved = sorted(n for n in n_markers if n not in biblio_nums)
    return {
        "leaked_cite_ev_tokens": len(leaked_cites),
        "leaked_cite_samples": sorted(set(leaked_cites))[:10],
        "distinct_bib_markers_in_prose": len(n_markers),
        "bibliography_entries": len(biblio_nums),
        "unresolved_markers": unresolved,
    }


# ── FLYWHEEL Rank4 — ARM THE SEMANTIC TOPIC JUDGE ON A PRE-BUILT CORPUS ─────────────────────────
#
# THE GAP (measured, not theorised): the §-1.3.1(b) off-topic DELETE leg has never fired on this
# path. `is_row_deletable_offtopic` keys on an AFFIRMATIVE ``topic_off_subject`` stamp, and the ONLY
# writer of that stamp is ``classify_topic_relevance`` — which runs at RETRIEVAL time (the live
# sweep) and is called by NOTHING on the pre-built-corpus path. The corpus carries 633
# ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` labels but ZERO ``topic_off_subject`` stamps, so the run logged
# "DELETED 0 judge-confirmed off-topic item(s)" and ~294 alien sources (climate-risk IMF working
# papers, privacy-attack bibliographies, school-infrastructure pages, content-marketing stats) sat in
# the grounding pool, eligible to be CITED in an AI-labour literature review. The faithfulness engine
# cannot catch them: strict_verify checks span fidelity, not topicality — a sentence grounded on a
# climate working paper is perfectly FAITHFUL and perfectly off-topic.
#
# THE FIX: ARM the judge that already exists. We do NOT build a new one, and we do NOT lexically
# guess. §-1.3.1(b) admits exactly one delete trigger — an affirmative SEMANTIC judge verdict,
# FAIL-OPEN. Everything below is fail-open scaffolding around that one call:
#   * The judge is run against ``rq`` — the SAME (DRB-override) question the report is composed and
#     scored against. Judging the corpus's own RQ would produce a FOREIGN verdict (the false
#     hard-drop §-1.3.1(b) forbids, and the exact landmine Rank2b defused).
#   * TOKEN-STARVATION TRAP (the silent no-op): ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS`` defaults to 1200, but
#     the judge model is reasoning-first — it burns the budget on hidden reasoning, truncates before
#     emitting its verdict lines, returns empty content, and EVERY batch fails open. The judge would
#     "run", bill real money, and delete nothing. We raise the floor to 4000 and FAIL LOUD if every
#     batch failed open (that is an outage, not a verdict of "all clean").
#   * BLAST-RADIUS CEILING: if the judge confirms OFF_SUBJECT on more than PG_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION
#     (default 0.40) of judged rows, we delete NOTHING and log LOUD. A well-formed but WRONG batch
#     response (index rotation, prompt injection from a snippet) is the one failure the parser cannot
#     catch; this is the containment. It can only ever REFUSE deletion — it can never force a number
#     up, so it is not a banned breadth knob.
#   * T1/T2 DOUBLE-CONFIRMATION (not exemption): a seminal paper wrongly verdicted OFF_SUBJECT is the
#     costliest false delete. A blanket high-tier exemption would use tier as a RELEVANCE proxy and
#     would shield precisely the credible-tier junk this audit found (the IMF climate WPs are T1/T2).
#     So tier is NOT a keep-veto; it is an ESCALATION criterion: a T1/T2 row the batch judge marks
#     OFF_SUBJECT is RE-judged alone on its full quote, and deletes only on a SECOND affirmative
#     verdict. Nobody is kept by tier and nobody is deleted by tier — the bar is just higher where a
#     mistake costs most.
# Default-OFF (``PG_PREBUILT_TOPIC_JUDGE``): unset => returns None => the generator passes ``()`` at
# the pool seam exactly as today => byte-identical.
_TOPIC_JUDGE_ENV = "PG_PREBUILT_TOPIC_JUDGE"
_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION_ENV = "PG_TOPIC_DELETE_MAX_FRACTION"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# W6 — the SHIPPING VEHICLE: wire the offline reflow (scripts/reflow_report.py) in-line.
#
# The reflow was built and proven as a report -> report transform over BANKED artifacts. Until it
# runs inside the composer it can never reach a scored run, so this is the vehicle — and nothing
# more: it is DEFAULT OFF and it is a NO-OP when off.
#
# House rule (Rank11's flag fired and consolidated ZERO): default-off is not enough — the ON path
# must PROVE IT BITES. So the ON path writes ``reflow_audit.json`` next to report.md with the
# structural deltas (H3s, bullets, tables, class-I kept, confessions purged), and it logs a
# BITE / NO-BITE verdict. A reflow that changed nothing says so, loudly, instead of taking credit.
#
# Faithfulness is NOT re-implemented here. The ON path calls ``reflow_report.reflow_markdown``,
# the exact function the CLI calls, so it inherits every validator and every fail-closed revert:

# 清洗文章的提示词，去除引用格式、参考文献等内容
clean_article_prompt_zh = """
<system_role>你是一名专业的文章编辑，擅长整理和清洗文章内容。</system_role>

<user_prompt>
下面给你的内容可能是一篇完整研究文章，也可能只是其中的一个连续片段（chunk）。
请只针对输入的这部分内容进行清洗，不要假设、补全或引用片段之外的上下文。

清洗目标：去除所有引用链接、引用标记（如[1]、[2]、1、2 等或其他复杂引用格式）、参考文献列表、脚注，并确保文章内容连贯流畅。
保留文章的所有其他原本内容、只移除引用。如果文章中使用引用标记中的内容作为语句的一部分，保留这其中的文字内容，移除其他标记。

特殊情况：如果输入的这段内容整段都属于某个文章的参考文献区（reference 列表 / 脚注定义等），不包含任何正文，请返回空字符串。

输入内容：
"{article}"

请返回清洗后的全文，不要添加任何额外说明或评论。
</user_prompt>
"""

clean_article_prompt_en = """
<system_role>You are a professional article editor who is good at cleaning and refining article content.</system_role>

<user_prompt>
The input below may be a complete research article or just a continuous fragment (chunk) of one.
Operate only on the content provided; do not assume, complete, or quote any context outside this fragment.

Cleaning goal: remove all citation links, citation marks (such as [1], [2], 1, 2, etc. or other complex citation formats), reference lists, footnotes, and ensure the content reads smoothly.
Keep all other original content; remove only citations. If a citation mark wraps content that is part of a sentence, keep the text inside and drop only the marks.

Special case: if the entire input is a reference / bibliography / footnote-definition section of some article with no body text, return an empty string.

Input:
"{article}"

Return the cleaned text in full, without any additional explanation or comment.
</user_prompt>
"""
